varieties.name: Species Form
varieties.origin: Taiwan, China, endemic to subtropical mountains
varieties.traits: Small to medium tree, 15-20 ft mature height. Five deeply lobed glossy leaves, smaller than Japanese maple.
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries in Brisbane/Queensland area, rare but available

varieties.name: 'Hot Blonde'
varieties.traits: Exceptionally heat tolerant, compact form

varieties.name: Species Form
varieties.origin: Eastern China, Taiwan, Japan, ancient cultivation
varieties.traits: Small to medium tree, 20-35 ft. Three-lobed triangular leaves resembling trident. Gray-brown exfoliating bark.

varieties.name: 'Aeryn' (Apollo)
varieties.traits: Upright oval form, red-yellow-orange fall color

varieties.name: 'Miyasama yatsubusa'
varieties.traits: Dwarf form, salmon to deep red new foliage

varieties.name: 'Bloodgood'
varieties.origin: Classic upright red, cultivated for over 50 years
varieties.traits: Upright form, 15-20 ft. Deep red-purple leaves hold color through summer.

varieties.name: 'Glowing Embers'
varieties.traits: Upright dense form, small leaves, 8-10 ft

varieties.name: 'Emperor 1' (Red Emperor)
varieties.traits: Deep maroon spring color, rich olive-green leaf undersides, brilliant scarlet fall

varieties.name: 'Seiryu'
varieties.traits: Upright dissectum (lacy leaves), vigorous grower, 20-22 ft, bright green foliage

varieties.name: 'Osakazuki'
varieties.traits: Upright form, green leaves in summer

varieties.name: 'Tamukeyama'
varieties.origin: Kobayashi Nursery, Japan, propagated for over 300 years
varieties.traits: Weeping red laceleaf, umbrella-like cascading form, 5-8 ft

varieties.name: 'Inaba shidare'
varieties.traits: Weeping laceleaf, vigorous, 5 ft x 6 ft in 10 years

varieties.name: 'Baton Rouge'
varieties.origin: Houston, Texas selection - extreme heat origin
varieties.traits: Spectacular bright red bark, larger leaves, red leaf edge tipping in spring, 10-12 ft

varieties.name: 'Hefner's Red'
varieties.traits: Upright dense form, deep maroon color, 12-15 ft in 20 years

varieties.name: 'Sango Kaku' (Coral Bark Maple)
varieties.traits: Magnificent coral-pink bark in winter, upright form

varieties.name: 'Ryusen'
varieties.origin: Pendulous Japanese maple with unique habit
varieties.traits: Weeping form, palmate foliage, amazing shape

varieties.name: 'Shishigashira' (Lion's Head)
varieties.traits: Compact, densely packed curled leaves, upright form

varieties.name: 'Kiyohime'
varieties.traits: Dwarf spreading form, 2-3 ft x 6-7 ft, tiny leaves with purple-red border

varieties.name: Species Form
varieties.origin: Endemic to north Queensland rainforests, first described 1866 by Ferdinand von Mueller
varieties.traits: Large evergreen tree, 35 m (115 ft) potential height. Pinnate leaves with 6-10 egg-shaped leaflets.
varieties.availability: Limited - protected species, available through specialty Australian native nurseries, Grow Billion Trees initiatives

varieties.name: Acer laevigatum (Smooth Maple, Nepal Maple)
varieties.origin: Southern China, northern India (Sikkim, Arunachal Pradesh), Myanmar, Nepal, northern Vietnam
varieties.traits: Atypical evergreen/semi-deciduous maple, bright red samaras in spring

varieties.name: Acer oblongum (Himalayan Maple, Evergreen Maple)
varieties.origin: Central, eastern, southeastern Asia - Tibet, northern India, Japan, China, Indochina
varieties.traits: Unique evergreen maple, stays green all winter, 15-22 m height

varieties.name: Acer campbellii (Campbell's Maple, Himalayan Maple)
varieties.origin: Bhutan, northern India, Laos, Myanmar, Nepal, Vietnam, China (Sichuan, Tibet, Yunnan)
varieties.traits: Deciduous tree, up to 15 m, large leaves 8-15 cm
